summary.intent: Add 100+ default bundled skills to Moltis (similar to Hermes Agent's skill library) with category organization, web UI visibility, and proper origin tracking.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented a bundled skills system embedding 100 vetted skills (from Hermes and OpenClaw) into the Moltis binary via include_dir!, with category metadata, web UI category filter, prompt format fallback, and full PR review cycle including security fixes.

repo: Moltis uses a worktree at ~/superset/worktrees/moltis/stealth-hovercraft for feature branches
Skills use SKILL.md frontmatter with fields: name, description, origin, requires (bins/any_bins/install), platforms — NOT metadata/dependencies/version/author like Hermes
SkillSource enum has variants: Personal, Project, Registry, Plugin — Bundled was added as new lowest-priority variant
Discovery priority: Project > Personal > Registry > Plugin > Bundled (user skills always override bundled by name)
Web UI /api/skills endpoint (crates/web/src/api.rs) had its own FsSkillDiscoverer instance separate from the gateway, requiring separate fix to show bundled skills
skill_detail RPC handler in services.rs has per-source branches — bundled source required its own branch to enable click-to-view in web UI
Hermes skills have sidecar directories (references/, templates/, scripts/, assets/) alongside SKILL.md — 53 of 67 skills have sidecars
mlops category has 3-level nesting (mlops/training/axolotl/) requiring recursive directory discovery
6 Hermes skills had directory names that didn't match frontmatter name field — caused silent read failures until tests caught it
style.css at crates/web/src/assets/css/style.css is generated Tailwind output — resolve merge conflicts by regenerating, not manual editing
crates/web crate needed its own bundled-skills feature entry and forwarding from CLI to expose bundled skills via /api/skills

code.finding: New module: BundledSkillStore with include_dir! embedding, dev-mode filesystem fallback, recursive discovery for nested categories, find_skill_dir for read-by-name
CompositeSkillDiscoverer merges FS + bundled sources; deduplication must use seen.insert() (not !seen.contains()) to catch intra-bundled duplicates
Three-tier format fallback: full (default 30KB budget) → compact self-closing tags → truncated prefix; header changes to signal compact mode
ReadSkillTool needs with_bundled() constructor; read_bundled() must call normalize_relative_skill_file_path() before passing to store to prevent path traversal
skill_detail handler needs explicit Bundled branch calling skill_detail_bundled(); otherwise clicking bundled skills in web UI silently does nothing
Exhaustive match on SkillSource — adding Bundled variant requires no-op arm here (bundled skills are not watched for filesystem changes)
CDN script tag needed SRI integrity hash — CodeQL flagged missing integrity attribute

workflow: Greptile bot posts automated code review comments on PRs — poll with gh api after push, then resolve threads via GraphQL minimizeComment mutation
Python sed-based frontmatter injection is fragile and can truncate files — use a Python script with proper file read/write instead
cargo check is sufficient to validate compilation without the disk-space cost of full cargo test on a worktree
Tests should verify directory name matches frontmatter name field — this caught 6 real bugs where read_skill() would silently fail
When adding a new enum variant to a Rust enum used in match expressions, search for all match sites (grep SkillSource::) before compiling
Node modules aren't installed in git worktrees — run npm install before tsc/vite builds
Background bash tasks via run_in_background may have output parsing issues with jq — always have a fallback direct gh api call

friction: sed-based frontmatter injection corrupted SKILL.md files by truncating content — had to re-copy from Hermes and use Python instead
Background bash task output had jq parse errors making it hard to read Greptile review results — required fallback direct API calls
Stash pop during merge created a second conflict on already-resolved style.css — required manual cleanup
cargo test on worktree failed with disk space errors due to llama-cpp compilation — had to use cargo check + per-crate tests instead
CompositeSkillDiscoverer was initially only deduplicating cross-source, not within bundled set itself — caught by Greptile review
Web UI /api/skills endpoint was completely separate from gateway's skill discovery — bundled skills appeared in system prompt but not the UI, requiring a second fix pass
Clicking bundled skills in the web UI silently did nothing because skill_detail RPC had no Bundled branch — discovered only after merge
open_items: Some Hermes body text references remain in godmode/SKILL.md ('Nous Hermes' model name references — intentionally kept as correct)
GitHub skills still have some ~/.hermes/ path references replaced with generic env patterns — could be further improved with Moltis-specific paths
OpenClaw platform-specific extensions (Feishu, QQBot, ACP) and internal skills (clawflow, clawhub) were skipped — could be revisited if Moltis gains those integrations
Format fallback compact mode truncation uses binary search — could be improved with smarter truncation that preserves higher-priority categories
No char budget configuration exposed to users yet — hardcoded 30KB default in generate_skills_prompt
~25 OpenClaw skills were not ported (openclaw-specific ones like oracle, canvas, voice-call, session-logs) — worth revisiting as Moltis gains equivalent features
Sidecar scripts (Python/shell) reference Hermes paths — scripts work as LLM reference but aren't directly executable without path fixes
